tools.name: ps_list_registered_tools
tools.title: List Photoshop Agent Tools
tools.description: Return the local ps-agent/v1 tool registry used by the MCP adapter.
tools.notes: Local introspection only; does not contact Photoshop or the Python backend.

tools.name: ps_ping_backend
tools.title: Ping Photoshop Agent Backend
tools.description: Check whether the Python backend is reachable and whether a UXP plugin heartbeat has been seen recently.

include_uxp.description: Include the latest UXP heartbeat state when available.

tools.notes: Use this before planning an edit to confirm the local bridge is alive.

tools.name: ps_bridge_diagnostics
tools.title: Get Photoshop Bridge Diagnostics
tools.description: Return backend, UXP heartbeat, SQLite queue, path, PID, token, model, and recent event diagnostics for bridge troubleshooting.
tools.notes: Use this when ps_ping_backend fails, UXP is stale, jobs are stuck, or previews/results are not returning.

tools.name: ps_list_capabilities
tools.title: List Photoshop Capabilities
tools.description: List the internal Photoshop capability catalog exposed through the existing ps-agent tool registry.
tools.notes: This is the registry-facing entrypoint for PS capabilities. Capabilities are not separate MCP tools unless promoted.

tools.name: ps_probe_capability
tools.title: Probe Photoshop Capability
tools.description: Return availability, safety, transport, and notes for one capability id.
tools.notes: Use before planning with an experimental or advanced capability.

tools.name: ps_validate_capability_call
tools.title: Validate Photoshop Capability Call
tools.description: Validate one capability call, including destructive and raw descriptor confirmations.
tools.notes: Codex must call this before advanced or destructive capabilities.

tools.name: ps_execute_capability
tools.title: Execute Photoshop Capability
tools.description: Execute a registered Photoshop capability through existing tools or the UXP job queue.
tools.notes: High-frequency features should still use dedicated tools. Low-frequency PS capabilities go through this generic executor. Raw batchPlay is advanced-only and requires confirmation.

tools.name: ps_scan_asset_library
tools.title: Scan Design Asset Library
tools.description: Scan the configured design asset folder and generate thumbnails/contact sheet for Codex visual review.
tools.notes: Default folder is D:\Photo_sontrol\design_assets\inbox or PS_AGENT_DESIGN_ASSET_ROOT. Original files are read-only; generated thumbnails live under backend/runtime/assets.

tools.name: ps_analyze_design_assets
tools.title: Analyze Design Assets
tools.description: Compute deterministic design-oriented metrics for scanned assets: dimensions, aspect ratio, alpha, colors, and suggested roles.
tools.notes: Codex still performs semantic visual understanding from the contact sheet; this tool provides stable metrics only.

tools.name: ps_prepare_asset_variant
tools.title: Prepare Design Asset Variant
tools.description: Copy/crop/resize a source asset from the design asset folder into backend runtime assets for Photoshop placement.
tools.notes: Use before asset.place_embedded. The source asset must stay inside the configured design asset root.

tools.name: ps_create_design_plan
tools.title: Create Design Workflow Plan
tools.description: Create a Design Overlay v2 planning container with design_brief, design_lock, layer_graph, and staged workflow slots for posters, panels, collages, covers, cards, or commercial materials.
tools.notes: Design is a planning overlay, not a separate execution universe. Codex owns design_brief/layer_graph decisions; execution must lower into shared operation_recipe or selection_recipe atoms.

tools.name: ps_validate_design_plan
tools.title: Validate Design Workflow Plan
tools.description: Validate a Design Overlay v2 plan, including design_lock completeness, layer_graph node/edge references, rollback targets, and atom executability.
tools.notes: Stages without operation_recipe or selection_recipe are allowed as planning stages. Executable stages must use known non-planned shared atoms, or lower valid layer_graph nodes first.

tools.name: ps_compile_svg_object
tools.title: Compile SVG Vector Object
tools.description: Compile one visual-style-layered vector object into deterministic SVG assets and an operation_recipe fragment without modifying Photoshop.
tools.notes: Inspect visual-layer sharding, SVG hashes, complexity, and lowered placement/group steps before Photoshop execution.

tools.name: ps_lower_design_stage_to_operation_recipe
tools.title: Lower Design Stage
tools.description: Lower one Design Overlay v2 stage into a shared operation_recipe or selection_recipe without making Photoshop changes.
tools.notes: This is a structural lowerer only. It never performs aesthetic choices and never emits raw Photoshop JavaScript.

tools.name: ps_apply_design_stage
tools.title: Apply Design Stage
tools.description: Execute exactly one design stage by lowering it into a shared operation_recipe or selection_recipe.
tools.notes: Apply exactly one stage, then export preview/crops and review before continuing. Operation stages queue operation_recipe jobs; selection stages use the shared selection_recipe path.

tools.name: ps_review_design_stage
tools.title: Review Design Stage
tools.description: Return review checklist and feedback mapping for a completed design stage.
tools.notes: Visual judgment remains Codex/user-driven; this tool standardizes review mapping.

tools.name: ps_export_design_package
tools.title: Export Design Package
tools.description: Return export defaults and output root for design deliverables; first implementation delegates flattened export to ps_export_preview.
tools.notes: Use returned export_preview_payload with ps_export_preview for flattened preview/export in v1.

tools.name: ps_list_operation_atoms
tools.title: List Operation Atoms
tools.description: List low-level Photoshop operation components for design, layer, asset, text, shape, filter, adjustment, mask, selection-clear, and preview recipes.
tools.notes: Use this before composing open-ended layer/effect recipes. Atoms are capabilities, not aesthetic presets.

tools.name: ps_list_selection_atoms
tools.title: List Selection And Mask Atoms
tools.description: List native, geometry, local-model, channel/alpha, and refinement selection components with hard-selection and soft-alpha bus metadata.
tools.notes: Use this during mask_strategy and mask_preparation. Selection atoms are equal-status candidates; do not hard-route sky/subject/object classes to one atom.

tools.name: ps_validate_operation_recipe
tools.title: Validate Operation Recipe
tools.description: Validate a sequence of low-level Photoshop operation atoms before UXP execution.
tools.notes: Codex must validate operation recipes before applying them. Recipes should create or group stage layers for rollback.

tools.name: ps_validate_selection_recipe
tools.title: Validate Selection Recipe
tools.description: Validate a candidate-based selection/mask recipe, including hard-selection vs soft-alpha bus rules and review requirements.
tools.notes: Complex local edits must trial and review candidate masks before lowering them to ps_make_selection, ps_apply_mask_to_layer, ps_apply_plan, or ps_apply_operation_recipe.

tools.name: ps_apply_operation_recipe
tools.title: Apply Operation Recipe
tools.description: Execute a validated operation atom recipe in Photoshop through UXP inside one executeAsModal history state.
tools.notes: This is the default executor for open-ended layer stacks such as blur blends, glow layers, grouped effects, and masks. The recipe must be Codex-planned, not selected from a fixed preset.

tools.name: ps_retouch_spot_heal_points
tools.title: Retouch Spot Heal Points
tools.description: Non-destructively remove Codex-reviewed blemish points by duplicating a source layer and running Photoshop content-aware fill over soft elliptical point selections.

source_layer_id.description: Optional source pixel layer id. Defaults to active layer.

name.description: Name for the duplicated retouch layer.

tools.notes: Use after Codex reviews a local crop and identifies blemish coordinates in document pixels. The tool creates a separate retouch layer; if the result is bad, delete or hide that layer/stage rather than touching the original.

tools.name: ps_retouch_content_aware_fill_selection
tools.title: Retouch Content-Aware Fill Selection
tools.description: Non-destructively run Photoshop content-aware fill on the current active selection, usually after a bbox/polygon/color/landmark mask has been reviewed.

source_layer_id.description: Optional source pixel layer id. Defaults to active layer.

target_layer_id.description: Optional target layer id when duplicate_source=false.

tools.notes: Requires an active Photoshop selection. Use for larger dust spots, scratches, or manually reviewed polygons/bboxes; small face blemishes are usually better handled by ps_retouch_spot_heal_points.

tools.name: ps_create_region_artifact
tools.title: Create Region Artifact
tools.description: Normalize face-landmarker, SAM/HQ-SAM alpha masks, polygons, bboxes, landmarks, or manual points into a unified region_artifact with multiple representations.
tools.notes: This is the bridge from region understanding to Photoshop expressions. It does not execute selection or edits; it creates a reusable region_artifact that can lower to alpha mask, polygon lasso, or path representation.

tools.name: ps_extract_region_contour
tools.title: Extract Region Contour
tools.description: Extract an approximate polygon contour and bezier path representation from a full-document alpha mask PNG.
tools.notes: Use when a soft alpha mask needs to be reused as polygon lasso or future pen/path input. The contour is an approximation for geometric operations; keep alpha masks for high-quality soft-edge edits.

tools.name: ps_lower_region_to_selection_recipe
tools.title: Lower Region To Selection Recipe
tools.description: Lower a region_artifact into an executable selection_recipe using alpha_mask, polygon, or bbox representation.
tools.notes: The returned selection_recipe should still be validated and applied with ps_apply_selection_recipe. Prefer alpha_mask for soft-edge edits; prefer polygon for lasso/marching-ants confirmation.

tools.name: ps_lower_region_to_path
tools.title: Lower Region To Pen Path
tools.description: Lower a region_artifact into a bezier path/path_recipe representation for future Photoshop pen/work-path, vector-mask, stroke, or path-text tools.
tools.notes: First version outputs path data only. It is the stable bridge from alpha/landmark/polygon regions to pen-path planning; native Photoshop path creation will be bound to a path atom later.

tools.name: ps_apply_selection_recipe
tools.title: Apply Selection Recipe
tools.description: Execute a validated selection/mask recipe. Hard-selection recipes run in UXP; soft-alpha recipes are composited in the backend into one alpha mask.
tools.notes: Use in mask_preparation. Hard recipes leave a current selection and save a final alpha channel. Soft recipes return a single alpha_mask selection_mask for downstream tools.

tools.name: ps_review_selection_recipe
tools.title: Review Selection Recipe
tools.description: Return review guidance that maps candidate mask issues back to selection recipe candidate ids and atom ids.
tools.notes: Use after overlay/crop review to decide whether to accept, tune, combine, or retry a mask candidate.

tools.name: ps_apply_mask_to_layer
tools.title: Apply Mask To Layer
tools.description: Apply a current selection, composite selection, or full-document alpha mask as a layer mask on a target layer.
tools.notes: Prefer this for soft alpha masks so the edge is preserved as a layer mask. ps_make_selection remains useful for marching-ants confirmation.

tools.name: ps_create_selection_strategy
tools.title: Create Selection Strategy
tools.description: Create an abstract, flexible selection/mask strategy with multiple equal-status candidate methods, refinement ideas, and review criteria before trying or applying a mask.

target.description: Target region or object to select, such as sky, blackboard, building, face, clothing, background, or color/tone range.

research_findings.description: Concise findings from web search/RAG about which Photoshop selection methods may work better for this target.

tools.notes: Use before complex mask_preparation stages. The output is abstract strategy, not executable selection_mask DSL. Do not emit concrete selection_mask.source values at this stage; Codex must inspect previews, use web/RAG findings, try plausible candidates, compare overlays/crops, then lower the winning candidate into a concrete mask only for ps_make_selection or ps_apply_plan.

tools.name: ps_validate_selection_strategy
tools.title: Validate Selection Strategy
tools.description: Validate that a selection strategy avoids fixed routing, contains candidate methods, review outputs, comparison policy, and add/subtract/intersect refinement rules.
tools.notes: Complex selection workflows should validate this before running tool trials. Strategy candidates must stay abstract and must not contain executable selection_mask payloads. Alpha masks remain soft and should be composited in backend; Photoshop boolean add/subtract/intersect is for native/bbox/polygon/current selections after a candidate is lowered.

tools.name: ps_create_workflow_plan
tools.title: Create Staged Workflow Plan
tools.description: Create a staged workflow skeleton for complex Photoshop tasks before any execution.
tools.notes: Codex remains the strategy owner; this creates the structured workflow container. Stage route examples are skeletons, not fixed templates. For route=photo_effect, named looks such as white-soft, bloom, halation, film mist, or Tyndall light belong in effect_intent only and must be lowered into concrete operation_recipe/selection_recipe/layer_recipe/plan payloads before execution.

tools.name: ps_validate_workflow_plan
tools.title: Validate Staged Workflow Plan
tools.description: Validate that each workflow stage has objective, expected result, review policy, pass criteria, and rollback target.
tools.notes: Complex edit workflows should pass this before stage execution. route=photo_effect stages may contain effect_intent, but effect_intent is strategy-only; executable stages must contain concrete steps/ops through operation_recipe, layer_recipe, selection_recipe, or a validated ps-agent/v1 plan.

tools.name: ps_apply_workflow_stage
tools.title: Apply One Workflow Stage
tools.description: Apply exactly one stage from a staged workflow plan and return stage tracking metadata.
tools.notes: Never use this to run a whole complex edit at once. Execute and review one stage at a time. photo_effect is a stage route, not a Photoshop operation; this tool only applies a lowered concrete recipe or plan.

tools.name: ps_review_workflow_stage
tools.title: Review Workflow Stage
tools.description: Return stage-level review criteria and feedback mapping for a completed workflow stage.
tools.notes: Feedback must map back to workflow_id -> stage_id -> step_id/primitive_id/capability_id.

tools.name: ps_finalize_workflow_review
tools.title: Finalize Workflow Review
tools.description: Return final review checklist for cross-stage side effects and acceptance.
tools.notes: Use after all accepted stages are complete.

tools.name: ps_delete_workflow_stage
tools.title: Delete Workflow Stage Group
tools.description: Delete one specific staged workflow layer group without removing other stages.
tools.notes: Use group_name from ps_apply_workflow_stage result when available. Otherwise provide workflow_id, stage_id, and stage apply job_id.

tools.name: ps_analyze_image_metrics
tools.title: Analyze Image Metrics
tools.description: Read-only deterministic image observation: tone/color percentiles, compact luma/saturation/value/hue/RGB histograms, Lab, HSV hue bands, dominant colors, and risk flags for Codex planning.

asset_path.description: Absolute local image path to analyze.

regions.description: Optional bbox regions in original image pixels.

tools.notes: Read-only deterministic observation layer. It must not generate Photoshop plans or parameter presets; Codex uses the metrics and histograms as evidence while writing its own staged plan.

tools.name: ps_retrieve_style_guidance
tools.title: Retrieve Style Guidance
tools.description: Retrieve local RAG style cards for a user goal, Codex visual brief, scene tags, and optional image metrics.
tools.notes: RAG guidance only provides style constraints, aliases, mask guidance, selected_reason, and failure traps; it must not directly generate Photoshop descriptors. Direct visual grading should use this as guidance only.

tools.name: ps_score_grade_preview
tools.title: Score Grade Preview
tools.description: Experimental helper that scores before/after/reference previews using deterministic metrics for reference similarity, subject protection, tone safety, color consistency, and black/white risk.
tools.notes: Experimental. Use only as an auxiliary signal alongside Codex visual review; do not let it decide whether a broad style grade is acceptable.

tools.name: ps_list_effect_primitives
tools.title: List Effect Primitives
tools.description: List the open-ended composable effect primitive library used to build layer recipes. These are visual mechanisms, not fixed presets.
tools.notes: Use before open-ended effect planning to inspect available visual mechanisms, safe ranges, and failure modes.

tools.name: ps_retrieve_effect_primitives
tools.title: Retrieve Effect Primitives
tools.description: Retrieve candidate effect primitives for a user goal, visual brief, scene tags, and avoid list.
tools.notes: Returns primitives, selection reasons, protection hints, review regions, and failure modes. It should not be treated as a preset matcher.

tools.name: ps_generate_layer_recipe
tools.title: Generate Layer Recipe
tools.description: Generate an open-ended layer recipe from selected effect primitives or by retrieving primitives from the user goal and visual brief.
tools.notes: v1 lowers primitives to already-supported ps_apply_plan operations while preserving primitive ids and failure modes for review.

tools.name: ps_validate_layer_recipe
tools.title: Validate Layer Recipe
tools.description: Validate that a layer recipe uses supported primitive-backed operations and safe layer settings.
tools.notes: Also validates the lowered ps_apply_plan when possible.

tools.name: ps_apply_layer_recipe
tools.title: Apply Layer Recipe
tools.description: Apply a validated primitive-backed layer recipe by lowering it to the existing non-destructive ps_apply_plan execution path.
tools.notes: v1 does not yet create stamp/blur/glare pixel layers; it uses stable adjustment-layer and Camera Raw operations while preserving primitive metadata.

tools.name: ps_review_layer_recipe
tools.title: Review Layer Recipe
tools.description: Return primitive-level review guidance, failure modes, and feedback mapping for a layer recipe.
tools.notes: Use after preview export so visual issues can be mapped back to primitive ids instead of vague whole-image feedback.

tools.name: ps_get_state
tools.title: Get Photoshop Document State
tools.description: Queue a UXP job that reads the active Photoshop document, dimensions, mode, active layer, revision, and optionally layer metadata.
tools.notes: This is the first tool Codex should call before generating an edit plan.

tools.name: ps_export_preview
tools.title: Export Full Preview
tools.description: Queue a UXP job that exports a scaled full-document preview for global visual evaluation.
tools.notes: Preview coordinates must report scale_factor so Codex can map visible pixels back to document pixels.

tools.name: ps_export_regions
tools.title: Export Local Regions
tools.description: Queue a UXP job that exports one or more bbox crops in document-pixel coordinates for local evaluation.

max_side.description: Target maximum output side for each region crop. Crops are scaled up or down so the longest side reaches this value.

upscale_small_regions.description: When true, crops smaller than max_side are upscaled for easier inspection and manipulation.

tools.notes: All bboxes use top-left origin and original document pixels.

tools.name: ps_generate_face_selection
tools.title: Generate Face Polygon Selection
tools.description: Generate ps-agent/v1 polygon selection masks for face parts from a local face crop using the optional local MediaPipe Face Landmarker model.

asset_path.description: Absolute local path of a face crop asset previously exported by ps_export_regions.

scale_factor.description: Scale factor from document pixels to the exported crop pixels.

face_index.description: Optional face index. If omitted, the largest detected face is used.

tools.notes: Face-region selections should prefer this tool. Codex-generated polygon coordinates are primarily for non-face regions and only a fallback for face when the local model is unavailable.

tools.name: ps_generate_sam_mask
tools.title: Generate SAM Alpha Mask
tools.description: Generate a full-document alpha mask PNG from an exported preview/crop using the optional local SAM 2.1 Base+ worker.

asset_path.description: Absolute local path of an exported preview or crop asset under the workspace.

document_bbox.description: The asset crop bbox in original Photoshop document pixels. For a full preview, use x=0,y=0,width=document width,height=document height.

scale_factor.description: Scale factor from document pixels to asset pixels.

prompt.description: First version supports bbox and positive/negative point prompts only. No text grounding.

tools.notes: Requires the optional SAM worker: python backend/cli.py sam start. Output selection_mask.source=alpha_mask should be used as a replace mask in ps_make_selection or ps_apply_plan. For add/subtract/intersect behavior, generate a new composited alpha mask rather than combining soft masks in Photoshop.

tools.name: ps_detect_grounding_boxes
tools.title: Detect Grounding Boxes
tools.description: Run local Grounding DINO text-guided detection on an exported preview or crop and return candidate boxes plus an overlay preview.
tools.notes: Requires the optional Grounding DINO + HQ-SAM worker: python backend/cli.py grounding start. Use this before segmentation when the target is a semantic object such as building, product, sign, flower, blackboard, or clothing.

tools.name: ps_generate_hqsam_mask
tools.title: Generate HQ-SAM Alpha Mask
tools.description: Generate a full-document alpha mask PNG from an exported preview or crop using the optional local HQ-SAM worker.
tools.notes: Requires the optional Grounding DINO + HQ-SAM worker: python backend/cli.py grounding start. Output selection_mask.source=alpha_mask can be consumed directly by ps_make_selection or ps_apply_plan.

tools.name: ps_generate_grounded_hq_mask
tools.title: Generate Grounded HQ Mask
tools.description: Run Grounding DINO detection and HQ-SAM refinement, then merge the instance masks into one full-document alpha mask PNG.
tools.notes: Default route for semantic non-face local masks such as buildings, products, signs, blackboards, flowers, clothing, and background objects. Soft add/subtract/intersect behavior is composed in the backend before Photoshop consumes the final alpha mask.

tools.name: ps_make_selection
tools.title: Make Active Photoshop Selection
tools.description: Queue a selection_mask job that creates the active Photoshop selection without creating layers or applying edits.

selection_mask.description: A ps-agent/v1 selection_mask object. Supports bbox, polygon, alpha_mask, current_selection, select_subject, select_sky, color_range, and composite sources.

tools.notes: Use this when the user asks to establish or refine an active Photoshop selection. It leaves the selection active for manual edits or later current_selection-based tools.

tools.name: ps_select_subject
tools.title: Photoshop Select Subject
tools.description: Use Photoshop's native Select Subject command to create an active selection.
tools.notes: Photoshop native AI selection; best for subject/product/person whole-object selection, not clothing-specific parsing.

tools.name: ps_select_sky
tools.title: Photoshop Select Sky
tools.description: Use Photoshop's native Select Sky command to create an active sky selection.
tools.notes: Photoshop native sky selection for landscape and outdoor images.

tools.name: ps_select_color_range
tools.title: Photoshop Color Range Selection
tools.description: Create an active selection with Photoshop's native Color Range command using sampled RGB or a named color preset.
tools.notes: Descriptor may need calibration on some Photoshop builds. If rejected, capture Action.json for Color Range.

tools.name: ps_extract_tonal_range
tools.title: Extract Tonal Range Selection
tools.description: Create an active Photoshop selection from highlights, midtones, or shadows without hand-writing a Color Range preset.
tools.notes: A higher-level wrapper over Color Range for the three most common tonal extractions: highlights, midtones, and shadows.

tools.name: ps_select_highlights
tools.title: Select Highlights
tools.description: Create an active selection from Photoshop highlights.
tools.notes: Convenience tonal extraction tool for bloom, white-soft, glare, and highlight protection setups.

tools.name: ps_select_midtones
tools.title: Select Midtones
tools.description: Create an active selection from Photoshop midtones.
tools.notes: Useful when the effect should live in the body of the image instead of only extreme highlights or shadows.

tools.name: ps_select_shadows
tools.title: Select Shadows
tools.description: Create an active selection from Photoshop shadows.
tools.notes: Convenience tonal extraction tool for haze, matte, fog, lifted-black, and shadow atmosphere control.

tools.name: ps_build_luminosity_mask
tools.title: Build Luminosity Mask Channel
tools.description: Create a highlights, midtones, or shadows selection and immediately save it into a named Photoshop alpha channel.
tools.notes: Sequential composite tool: tonal range selection -> optional selection modify steps -> save selection to alpha channel. Requires wait=true and a live UXP connection.

tools.name: ps_select_focus_area
tools.title: Photoshop Focus Area Selection
tools.description: Attempt Photoshop's native Focus Area command to create a selection from in-focus regions.
tools.notes: Experimental descriptor wrapper. Useful for shallow depth-of-field photos after descriptor calibration.

tools.name: ps_modify_selection
tools.title: Modify Active Photoshop Selection
tools.description: Apply Photoshop selection modification commands: feather, expand, contract, smooth, or border.
tools.notes: Requires an active selection. Good for cleaning model-generated or Photoshop-generated selections before applying masks.

tools.name: ps_save_selection_channel
tools.title: Save Selection To Alpha Channel
tools.description: Save the current active Photoshop selection into a named alpha channel.
tools.notes: Use after a good selection is established so it can be reloaded later.

tools.name: ps_load_selection_channel
tools.title: Load Selection From Alpha Channel
tools.description: Load a named alpha channel as the active Photoshop selection.
tools.notes: Use to restore a named saved selection before applying an edit plan to current_selection.

tools.name: ps_selection_command
tools.title: Run Photoshop Selection Command
tools.description: Run a whitelisted Photoshop native selection command, including select_all, deselect, inverse, select_subject, select_sky, color_range, focus_area, modify, save_selection, and load_selection.
tools.notes: Convenience wrapper for menu-like selection actions. Prefer dedicated tools when available; use this for select_all, deselect, and inverse.

tools.name: ps_validate_plan
tools.title: Validate Edit Plan
tools.description: Validate a ps-agent/v1 edit plan against backend safety rules without changing Photoshop, including PS 25 composite selection masks and experimental acr_ai_mask targets when explicitly enabled.
tools.notes: Codex must call this before ps_apply_plan. selection_mask supports operation=replace/add/subtract/intersect for bbox, polygon, color_range, select_subject, select_sky, and source=composite ordered items. source=color_range can be used multiple times with different presets/colors/fuzziness thresholds, then refined by subtracting subject/foreground selections. source=alpha_mask is replace-only and preserves soft mask edges from SAM/other mask generators. Global camera_raw_filter is the preferred main color-grading path. Non-destructive adjustment ops include adjust_hue_saturation, adjust_curves, adjust_levels, adjust_selective_color, adjust_gradient_map, and adjust_color_lookup. Experimental acr_ai_mask targets require plan.safety.allow_experimental_acr_masks=true; camera_raw_internal currently reports availability explicitly, while photoshop_selection_fallback works for subject/background/sky.

tools.name: ps_apply_plan
tools.title: Apply Edit Plan
tools.description: Queue a validated ps-agent/v1 plan for UXP execution in Photoshop using non-destructive adjustment layers including Hue/Saturation, Curves, Levels, Selective Color, Gradient Map, Color Lookup, alpha masks, PS 25 composite selection masks, smart objects, whitelisted Camera Raw smart filters, and gated experimental ACR AI mask targets.

plan.description: A ps-agent/v1 edit plan that already passed validation.

tools.notes: The UXP side executes document mutations inside executeAsModal. selection_mask source=alpha_mask downloads a full-document transparent PNG and loads its transparency as a soft selection before creating the adjustment layer mask. source=bbox/polygon uses PS 25 Selection API SelectionType REPLACE/EXTEND/DIMINISH/INTERSECT; source=color_range/select_subject/select_sky can also participate in composite add/subtract/intersect through temporary alpha-channel loading; source=composite applies ordered replace/add/subtract/intersect items. Use global camera_raw_filter for main color grading. Use adjust_hue_saturation, adjust_curves, adjust_levels, adjust_selective_color, adjust_gradient_map, and adjust_color_lookup for editable Photoshop adjustment layers; all can be constrained by selection_mask targets except camera_raw_filter's global smart-filter path. Experimental acr_ai_mask supports camera_raw_internal capability reporting and a working photoshop_selection_fallback for subject/background/sky.

tools.name: ps_delete_agent_group
tools.title: Delete Agent Layer Group
tools.description: Delete one specific Codex Agent layer group by the apply job id without using Photoshop history undo.

job_id.description: Apply job id whose group name is exactly Codex Agent - {job_id}.

tools.notes: Preferred cleanup path for a known agent apply job. The UXP side constructs the fixed group name Codex Agent - {job_id}, verifies it is a layer group, and deletes only that group.

tools.name: ps_undo_last
tools.title: Undo Last Agent Edit
tools.description: Queue a UXP job that rolls back the latest Codex/agent-created Photoshop edit where possible.

job_id.description: Optional job id to undo. If omitted, undo the latest agent edit.

tools.notes: Use ps_delete_agent_group for a known apply job id. Keep this as a fallback only when Photoshop history rollback is explicitly needed.

tools.name: ps_get_job
tools.title: Get Job Result
tools.description: Read a queued/running/completed job record from the Python backend.
tools.notes: Used when ps_apply_plan or export tools run asynchronously.
